source.title: BSEE Accident Investigation Report - Structural Damage, Horn Mountain SPAR

actions_taken: Notification made to New Orleans District after-hours engineer at 1900 hours
Fallen chain jack and nylon slings sent to Broussard Storage Facility for examination
Remaining jacks and previously sent jack sent to Solution Services in Houston for lifting pad-eye repair
New lift plan developed using longer choker slings
Re-installation of chain jacks 7-8-9 completed successfully with new plan
event.summary: On 21 October 2023 at 13:04 hours at Mississippi Canyon 127 A, Horn Mountain platform, personnel attempted a heavy lift of 8,300 pounds using the platform's West Crane. When the lift was 15 feet above the platform deck, two nylon choker slings failed due to contact with a sharp edge on the hydraulic chain jack cylinder, causing the load to fall onto two cargo baskets. No personnel injuries occurred. The root cause was the decision to use nylon choker slings wrapped around the cylinder body instead of the manufacturer-recommended lifting pad-eyes, combined with inadequate hazard analysis that failed to identify the sharp edge as a hazard.
recommendations: OII should draft safety alert to inform operators to utilize proper lifting techniques as recommended by the manufacturer

hazards.name: Hydraulic chain jack cylinder with sharp edges
Heavy lifting operations with suspended loads

threats.name: Nylon sling contact with sharp edge on jack cylinder
Inadequate sling positioning and sizing for load geometry
Failure to use manufacturer-recommended lifting pad-eyes

consequences.name: Potential personnel injury from dropped object

controls.name: Use of manufacturer-recommended lifting pad-eyes
controls.barrier_role: Primary lifting mechanism to prevent sling contact with sharp edges
controls.lod_basis: Manufacturer design specification for safe lifting

supporting_text: The manufacturer of the hydraulic chain jacks recommends the use of the lifting pad-eyes when lifting, removing, and installing the jacks.

The LA's made the decision not to use the dedicated lifting pad-eyes on each jack. The LA's stated that they did not believe the pad-eyes were fully functional, as they had not been used in many years, and were believed to be frozen up.
Utilizing the pad-eyes prevents the slings from contacting any portion on the jack which may compromise the integrity of the slings.

controls.name: Job Safety Analysis (JSA) hazard identification
controls.barrier_role: Identify sharp edges and other hazards that could compromise sling integrity
controls.lod_basis: Standard safety management practice for non-routine lifting operations

supporting_text: The JSA did not identify the sharp edge near the top of the jack as a hazard for the lifting operation.

controls.name: Lifting pad-eye refurbishment and maintenance
controls.barrier_role: Ensure lifting pad-eyes are functional and available for use
controls.lod_basis: Equipment maintenance and readiness for safe lifting operations

supporting_text: When the hydraulic chain jacks were originally sent in to BSF for maintenance, the decision was made not to refurbish the lifting pad-eyes prior to returning offshore.

controls.name: Sling selection and sizing for load geometry
controls.barrier_role: Select appropriately sized slings that prevent contact with sharp edges
controls.lod_basis: Proper rigging practice and load control procedures

supporting_text: The Choker slings were previously used to remove and re-install multiple chain jacks on the facility without incident. The slings that had been previously used were larger, longer, and bulkier than the slings that eventually failed. The slings used when the chain jack fell were smaller and shorter.
Because of this, the slings were placed in a position that would compromise them by being exposed to a sharp edge near the top of the jack.

controls.name: Personnel exclusion zone during lifting operations
controls.barrier_role: Prevent personnel injury from dropped objects
controls.lod_basis: Standard dropped object prevention and personnel safety procedures

supporting_text: There were no personnel near the area where/when the load fell. Prior to the lift being hoisted, personnel had moved away from the lift so as to not be impacted by possible dropped objects.

notes.rules: JSON output only. mentioned fields must be evidence-based. Use null for unknown values.
